root.title: Explore use case built on watsonx

watsonx-test.title: Here are the next steps to embed a chatbot with Watson Assistant
watsonx-test.subtitle: Use these tailored recommendations to try our sample chatbot or create your own using Watson Assistant.  Bookmark these results or go back through to explore additional options

items.desc: Refer to the blog for instructions on how to integrate Watson Assistant chatbot

items.title: Embed the chatbot you built into your product
items.desc: First, log into Watson Assistant and copy your chatbot code.  Paste it into the index file of your application.

items.title: Advanced integrations
items.desc: Add integrations to your assistant so that you can publish your bot to the channels where your customers go for help.

data.title: Phone
data.desc: Adding the phone integration to your assistant makes your assistant available to customers over the phone.

data.title: Slack
data.desc: Slack is a cloud-based messaging application that helps people collaborate with one another. After you create an action, you can integrate your assistant with Slack.

data.title: Facebook Messenger
data.desc: Facebook Messenger is a messaging application that helps businesses and customers communicate directly with one another. After you create an action, you can integrate your assistant with Facebook Messenger.

data.title: WhatsApp with Twilio
data.desc: Integrate with WhatsApp messaging so your assistant can exchange messages with your customers where they are.

data.title: SMS
data.desc: Enable customers to send text messages to your hosted phone number. When you create a phone number with Twilio, SMS message will be automatically supported.

data.title: Webchat
data.desc: To add the web chat widget to your website, all you need to do is embed a generated script element in your HTML source. The web chat integration is automatically included for every assistant, and is configured separately for the draft and live environments.

data.title: Search
data.desc: Put your subject matter expertise to work by adding search. The search integration gives your assistant access to corporate data collections that it can mine for answers.

guides.desc: Read the full Watson Assistant documentation
View Watson Assistant product details and pricing information.
Explore videos, tutorials, and articles for using Watson Assistant

watsonx-product.title: Speech to Text embedded library using a pre-trained model
watsonx-product.subtitle: Experience this embeddable library by seeing how it works, learning more about how it can be used, and then deploying it with your application.

items.desc: Please go through the following steps.

items.title: Step 1. Discover potential use cases
items.desc: Read how speech-to-text aids in achieving business goals.
items.modalDesc: The Watson Speech to Text Library transcribes written text from spoken audio. It leverages machine learning to combine knowledge of grammar, language structure, and the composition of audio and voice signals to accurately transcribe the human voice. It is ideal for applications that need to extract high-quality speech transcripts for use cases such as call centers, custom care, agent assistance, and similar solutions.

modalItems.title: Common Use Cases
data: Customer Self Service - Answer queries using Watson-powered virtual agent with voice support. Increase call resolution efficiency with increased automated responses.
Agent Assist - Capture discussion between caller and agent using Watson Speech real-time transcription. Reduce costs by leveraging contextual agent assist.
Contact Center Insights - Transcribe audio recordings of discussions between callers using Watson Speech for post-call transcription.

items.title: Step 2. Try the interactive demo application
items.desc:
Experience our interactive demo with a pre-trained model—explore using sample or your own data.
**Trying your own data will require an IBMid.**

items.title: Step 3. View code snippet
items.desc: This Jupyter notebook snippet shows you the basics of using the pre-trained model for text recognition, audio pre-processing, noise removal and number of speakers.

items.title: Step 4. Learn how to use the STT library in a container from local machine
items.desc: Learn step by step how to use Speech to Text notebook for text recognition, audio pre-processing, noise removal, and number of speakers with a local container and model. Make sure you get the entitlement key as mentioned in the document.

items.title: Step 5. See how to access the model from a client app
items.desc: Follow these steps in order to access your STT model over a REST API from a Java Spring Boot web application.
items.text: Use a client app to access an STT app

items.title: Step 6. Modify client application
items.desc: Dig deeper into the code behind the Spring Boot client application so that you can modify it for your needs.

items.title: Step 7. Learn more
items.desc: A quick overview deck of what is included in the assets and value prop of the accelerator, use-cases covered, implementation details, reference architecture and features.
Please go through the following steps.

items.title: Step 1. Get your entitlement key
items.desc: Get your entitlement key for embedding the model in a container. You will either need a trial key that enables you to use Watson Speech to Text for 180 days, or a production deployment key.
items.text: Get trial license and access container

items.title: Step 2. Apply for credits
items.desc: Apply for credits to use for API calls and optionally deploy your client application on the IBM Cloud. If you qualify, you can unlock $3,000 USD for 6 months.

items.title: Step3. View reference architecture
items.desc: Understand the the typical deployment scenario including how to run the model, access it via REST API, and connect it with Watson NLP AI capabilities.

items.title: Step 4. Build your application
items.desc: Build your application as a container and deploy it on Kubernetes, OpenShift, or Code Engine.
